characters.physical_details: Tall, weather-browned, ash-blond hair cut blunt with kitchen scissors, and wind-cracked hands from field labor.

key_relationships.august_poeschel: creditor and adversary through the bank note
key_relationships.hester_blum: older neighbor, confidante, and practical ally

characters.occupation_status: Widowed wheat farmer on the Vale place until the chapter 16 foreclosure sale; afterward displaced and heading east with Dell and Thea.
characters.literacy_languages: Reads and writes English competently and keeps household accounts.

state_transitions.change: Refuses Knapp's voluntary cooperation form, forcing August toward formal foreclosure.
Forces August to admit the basin deed date was changed after Ezra signed.
Puts Dell's survival ahead of saving the farm.
Chooses public truth at the auction; the bank takes the farm on a credit bid.
Buries Hester, scatters Otto's buffalo-grass seed at Saint's Basin, and boards the eastbound train with Dell and Thea.

characters.availability: Present throughout; at the Vale farm until the auction, then at Hester's bakery until departure.

characters.physical_details: Dark-haired, narrow-faced, usually neat until the weather defeats her, with careful clothes dulled by repeated dusting and mending.

key_relationships.august_poeschel: bank president whose paper power threatens her household and reputation
key_relationships.hester_blum: friend, later host, and moral witness

characters.occupation_status: Morrow schoolteacher until the school closes; Lincoln library applicant using Lincoln as the trio's first destination after the auction.
characters.literacy_languages: Highly literate in English and comfortable with correspondence, school records, official forms, and literary texts.

state_transitions.change: Receives the Lincoln library interview letter and replies yes.
Tears up and burns the Lincoln timetable, choosing the basin fight and Morrow over the immediate trip.
Closes the schoolhouse and finds Ezra's hidden letter plus his copy of Otto Blum's basin map in the hollowed geography book.
Leaves the Vale house for Hester's bakery after refusing to bury Ezra's letter.
Agrees with Cora and Dell on Lincoln first as the immediate route out of Morrow.
Boards the train east with Cora and Dell.

characters.availability: At the Vale farm through chapter 14; in Hester's bakery from chapter 14 to departure.

characters.physical_details: Lean, long-limbed, dust-light hair, quick hands, and a face that can go hard or unmistakably young.

key_relationships.thea_bell: former teacher and one of the adults who keeps trying to reach him
key_relationships.hester_blum: search partner and surrogate grandmother figure
key_relationships.august_poeschel: banker tied to his father's ruin

characters.occupation_status: Farm laborer and mechanic; earns small cash from repair work and plans to go west after Lincoln.
characters.literacy_languages: Reads and writes English well enough for schoolwork, manuals, letters, and timetables, even when he pretends otherwise.

state_transitions.change: Joins Hester's basin investigation instead of waiting for adults or weather to explain Ezra.
Confirms the tile line and diversion ditch on Vale land and understands the basin was deliberately cut.
Prys open the seep, saves Rudy Eckert from the collapse, breaks his left forearm, and badly bruises his ribs.
Realizes the adults' secrets include more than Ezra's bargain.
Hears Ezra's letter read publicly and loses the martyr version of his father.
Insists on his own say in the exit plan and commits to Lincoln first.
Opens Hester's blue-lid jar at Saint's Basin and leaves Morrow by train.

characters.availability: Present throughout; bedridden with fever in chapter 12 and in cast plus sling through the end of the cycle.

characters.physical_details: Heavyset, carefully shaved, and still carrying town authority in posture and clothes even when dust has gotten into both.

key_relationships.dell_vale: son of a debtor family and witness to August's failure
key_relationships.thea_bell: schoolteacher drawn into the Vale dispute
key_relationships.hester_blum: old community contemporary and persistent accuser
key_relationships.ezra_vale: dead debtor whose bargain he altered

characters.occupation_status: President of Farmers State Bank in Morrow and the town's de facto civic authority as formal institutions thin out.
characters.literacy_languages: Reads and writes formal English fluently and can still read older German family and church documents.

state_transitions.change: Cuts Cora's extension to a June 1 deadline under examiner pressure.
Tries to secure Cora's voluntary resettlement consent and moves toward formal foreclosure when she refuses.
Admits he changed the basin deed date after Ezra signed.
Gives Walter Knapp the true ledger leaf and signs a waiver of Cora's personal deficiency while keeping the sale on schedule.
Publicly admits the bank altered the effective date; the bank takes the Vale place on a credit bid.

characters.availability: Present throughout and remains in Morrow after the others leave.

characters.physical_details: Broad-handed, flour-dusted, stooped more by work than age, with a persistent dust cough and sharp eyes.

key_relationships.cora_vale: neighbor, elder ally, and practical witness to the Vale marriage
key_relationships.thea_bell: friend, later host, and moral witness
key_relationships.dell_vale: search partner and surrogate grandmother figure
key_relationships.august_poeschel: old community contemporary tied to the basin's betrayal

characters.occupation_status: Bakery owner, herbal fixer, and town memory-keeper.
characters.literacy_languages: Reads and writes English well, keeps recipes and weather notes, and can still sound out older German prayer books and family papers.

state_transitions.change: Proves jars can hold storm-dust memories and reopens Otto's original basin map.
Turns the bakery into the organizing point for tools, names, and witnesses for the basin dig.
Gives the jars and buffalo-grass seed to Cora, Dell, and Thea, and makes them agree on Lincoln first.
Dies before dawn and is buried beside Otto.

characters.availability: Present through early chapter 18; dies before the trio leaves town.

characters.physical_details: Seen only in memories: rangy, prematurely stooped, quick with his hands, and worn down in his last year.

key_relationships.hester_blum: neighbor who knew more of the basin history than he admitted

characters.occupation_status: Dead before chapter 1; former Vale farmer whose signed basin deed, hidden letter, and schoolbook map reshape the story.
characters.literacy_languages: Functional English reader and writer, capable of notes, labels, and the hidden confession letter.
characters.availability: Dead before the novel opens; present only through memory, documents, and testimony.

characters.physical_details: Dead before chapter 1; remembered as narrow-shouldered, deliberate, and often carrying survey stakes or grass seed.

key_relationships.august_poeschel: old community counterpart whose family ignored his basin warnings

characters.occupation_status: Dead before chapter 1; formerly a baker in winter and local survey hand and grass advocate in warmer seasons.
characters.literacy_languages: Reads, writes, and surveys in English; keeps field notes and map markings used after his death.
characters.availability: Dead before the novel opens; appears in jars, maps, and remembered basin work.

characters.physical_details: Dead before chapter 1; remembered only in family recollection and the blue-lid jar as a lively child with goose feathers on her stocking.
characters.occupation_status: Dead before chapter 1; formerly helped in the bakery and on relief sewing.
characters.literacy_languages: English not specified beyond ordinary local schooling.
characters.availability: Dead before the novel opens; present only in memory.

characters.physical_details: Trim, office-bred federal agent with a good hat, cedar brush, and careful clothes that never fully beat Nebraska dust.

key_relationships.cora_vale: official pressing voluntary resettlement or orderly foreclosure
key_relationships.thea_bell: educated local witness to Morrow's contraction

characters.occupation_status: Federal resettlement and title official reviewing Morrow's contraction.
characters.literacy_languages: Reads and writes formal English and federal paperwork fluently.

state_transitions.change: Offers the Vale place voluntary cooperation under resettlement pressure.
Takes the true ledger leaf and the signed original deficiency waiver out of August's custody.

characters.availability: Appears in chapters 4, 7, and 15; otherwise offstage.

timeline.story_start: Sunday, April 14, 1935, as Black Sunday hits Morrow and the Vale house.

seasonal_track.season: late spring into early summer, with repeated dust and the June deadline approaching
high summer into early autumn, with the basin dig, Dell's injury, school closure, and Ezra's hidden letter
autumn, with the auction, Hester's decline, burial, and rail departure

key_events.event: Black Sunday engulfs Morrow and the first visible dust-memory appears in the Vale window.
Hester traps storm dust in jars, proves the memories can be preserved, and reopens Otto's original basin map.
Cora refuses Knapp's voluntary cooperation form, pushing the bank toward formal foreclosure.
Saint's Basin is reopened; a seep appears and Dell is injured saving Rudy Eckert in the collapse.
Thea closes the schoolhouse and finds Ezra's hidden letter plus his copy of Otto's basin map in the hollowed geography book.
August gives Knapp the true Vale ledger page and signs a waiver of Cora's personal deficiency.
Ezra's letter is read aloud at the Vale auction; the bank's altered dating becomes public and the bank takes the farm on a credit bid.
Hester dies, the blue-lid jar is opened at Saint's Basin, buffalo-grass seed is scattered, and the trio leaves Morrow by rail toward Lincoln.

geography.primary_setting: Morrow, a dying wheat town in southwestern Nebraska; the Vale place lies west of town and Saint's Basin lies south of the west field.

key_locations.name: Vale farmstead
key_locations.details: Worn farmhouse, shed, chicken run, windmill, and failing wheat ground west of town; home until the chapter 16 foreclosure sale.

key_locations.name: Morrow schoolhouse
key_locations.details: Single-room school with blackboard memories; closes during chapter 13 and holds the hollowed geography book where Ezra hid his letter and map copy.

key_locations.name: Farmers State Bank
key_locations.details: Dust-filmed bank office with teller cage, August's back office, active-note cabinet, and vault.

key_locations.name: Hester Blum's bakery
key_locations.details: Bakery and living quarters where Hester keeps the jars; Thea moves here in chapter 14, and the trio shelters here after the auction.

key_locations.name: Saint's Basin
key_locations.details: Former wet meadow cut by tile and diversion ditch; reopened in chapter 11, posted as dangerous afterward, and still damp in chapter 18.

key_locations.name: Morrow cemetery
key_locations.details: North of the church; Otto and Hester are buried side by side here in chapter 18.

key_locations.name: Morrow depot and rail spur
key_locations.details: East-edge station and rail link; the mixed train that carries Cora, Dell, and Thea out of town leaves here in chapter 18.

distances: The Vale farm sits about two miles west of Morrow's main street.
Saint's Basin lies about a quarter mile south of the Vale west field and is reachable on foot from the farm.
The bakery, bank, schoolhouse, and post office sit within a few walkable blocks of each other in town.
The depot is roughly a mile east of the bakery and bank district.
Lincoln requires staged rail travel through larger junctions rather than a casual day trip.

world_rules: This is 1935 Nebraska: travel is slow, rail links matter, and credit plus county paperwork decide who keeps land.
Dust storms can black out noon, infiltrate closed houses, taint food and water, damage lungs, and strip crops.
After major dust events or when damp, dusted surfaces are disturbed, glass, enamel, mirrors, chalkboards, wet soil, seep water, and similar surfaces can display place-bound memories.
The same force records fragments of the immediate present as well as the past; it does not predict the future or invent events.
Dust memories are fragmentary, sensory, and local rather than full explanations.
Each sealed storm-dust jar preserves one imprint until it is opened and clearly witnessed.
Characters treat the phenomenon through weather lore, grief, faith, and practical need rather than formal magical language.
Rural 1935 Nebraska subjects widows, debtors, teachers, and queer relationships to intense communal scrutiny.

power_structure.holder: August Poeschel and Farmers State Bank
power_structure.over: Cora Vale and other debtors in Morrow
power_structure.mechanism: Notes, liens, deadline changes, foreclosure paperwork, and auction authority

power_structure.holder: Walter Knapp and federal resettlement authorities
power_structure.over: August Poeschel and Morrow's remaining landholders
power_structure.mechanism: Inspection reports, title review, and recommendations about contraction or abandonment

power_structure.holder: Morrow school board and the collapsing tax base
power_structure.mechanism: Salary, closure inventory, and employment continuity
Maternal authority, household labor expectations, and veto power over when he may leave
Reputation, employability, physical safety, and the livability of secrecy

objects.name: Antedated deed of security on Saint's Basin and its water claim
objects.owner: Farmers State Bank file until exposed
objects.origin: Ezra signs the basin security deed; August later scrapes and re-enters the effective date.
objects.status: Seen as an unnamed document in chapter 1, identified in chapter 9, and publicly implicated again at the chapter 16 auction.

objects.name: Otto Blum's original basin survey map
objects.origin: Otto's field map and runoff notes from before the basin was drained.
objects.status: Recovered in chapter 3 and used to locate the tile line and ditch in chapter 6.

objects.name: Ezra's copy of Otto Blum's basin map
objects.owner: Found by Thea, then carried with the trio's papers
objects.origin: Ezra's careful copy of Otto's basin marks and notes.
objects.status: Hidden in the hollowed geography book until chapter 13; used with the letter at the auction and packed for departure.

objects.name: Sealed storm-dust jars
objects.owner: Hester Blum until chapter 17; then Cora, Dell, and Thea
objects.origin: Dust skimmed after major storms; each jar keeps one imprint until opened.
objects.status: Crated in chapter 17; the blue-lid jar is opened at Saint's Basin in chapter 18 and the remaining jars leave Morrow on the train.

objects.name: Hollowed geography book
objects.owner: Morrow schoolhouse until found by Thea
objects.origin: A state geography cut and pasted into a hiding place for Ezra's letter and map copy.
objects.status: Opened in chapter 13 and no longer secret afterward.

objects.name: Ezra Vale's unsent letter
objects.owner: Hidden by Ezra, found by Thea, later carried with the trio's papers
objects.origin: Ezra's confession that he agreed to the basin cut first and that August later changed the deed date.
objects.status: Found in chapter 13, fought over in chapter 14, read aloud in chapter 16, and packed for departure.

objects.name: Lincoln library interview correspondence
objects.origin: An interview invitation from the library in Lincoln, followed by a later confirming letter from the librarian.
objects.status: Arrives in chapter 2, is reaffirmed in chapter 5, the first timetable is burned in chapter 8, and Lincoln remains the trio's first destination in chapters 17-18.

objects.name: Carbon copy of August's basin-date memorandum
objects.origin: August's copied written admission that the basin security date was entered after Ezra signed.
objects.status: Delivered before the auction, shown publicly in chapter 16, and carried out of Morrow with the household papers.

objects.name: Otto's buffalo-grass seed packet
objects.owner: Hester Blum until chapter 17; then Cora Vale
objects.origin: Native grass seed Otto saved from the basin edges before plow.
objects.status: Given to the trio in chapter 17 and scattered over the damp basin crescent in chapter 18.

debts.amount: $1,280 plus interest on the Vale note
debts.deadline: August cuts the extension to June 1 in chapter 4, then moves to formal foreclosure after Cora refuses cooperation in chapter 7.
debts.status: Foreclosed through the chapter 16 auction; August waives any personal deficiency in chapter 15.

debts.amount: $94 in back property taxes tied to the farm
debts.deadline: Payable by the next county collection cycle
debts.status: Rolled into the larger foreclosure pressure

debts.deadline: Monthly, increasingly flexible because trade is poor
debts.status: Managed by barter and delayed cash; still part of Hester's strain

income_sources.source: Cora's eggs, occasional chickens, sewing, and small household labor
income_sources.amount: small irregular cash and barter, never enough to carry the bank note alone

income_sources.source: Thea's school salary and board contribution
income_sources.amount: $52 per month when the board pays, plus the board money supporting the Vale household while she lives there
income_sources.reliability: medium until school closure, then gone

income_sources.amount: modest cash plus barter in bread, coffee, and herbal treatment

income_sources.source: Dell's repair work, threshing-belt sales, field labor, and similar trade jobs
income_sources.amount: sporadic cash, including small savings by chapter 17, then interrupted by injury
stable relative to the town, though dependent on institutional survival
income_sources.reliability: high in the novel's time frame

knowledge_state.knows: She and Thea are lovers; Dell knows by chapter 14, but the relationship remains unsafe to name openly in Morrow.
August altered the basin deed after Ezra signed, and Ezra himself first agreed to drain Saint's Basin.
Ezra's hidden letter and map copy prove that he agreed to cut the basin first and that August later changed the deed date.
He heard Ezra sobbing in the barn the night before the suicide and never woke Cora.
By chapter 14 he understands Cora and Thea are more than landlady and boarder, and by chapter 16 he knows Ezra signed the basin bargain before August altered it.
Morrow is already being mapped as a contraction or clearance problem, and by chapter 15 he also has the true Vale ledger leaf plus the original deficiency waiver.

environmental_constants: Dust sits inside food, bedding, hair, lungs, and speech; walls never fully solve the weather.
The soundscape alternates between abrasive wind, screen-door rasp, grit-packed rooms, and post-storm hush.
Hester's bakery smells of yeast, flour, coffee, herbs, medicine, and dust.
The bank smells of paper, metal, sweat, stale coffee, and dust caught in ledgers.
Saint's Basin, once reopened, smells of damp roots, iron, dead reeds, and blacker soil than the rest of the county.
Morrow's streets are sparse, boarded, and increasingly emptied by departure.
